title: inula britannica subsp. japonica
intro: Inula britannica subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese elecampane, is a subspecies of the elecampane plant, a perennial herb that belongs to the daisy family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils. The health benefits of Japanese elecampane include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Japanese elecampane is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its roots and rhizomes are also used in traditional medicine.
